ypt(companyId, RefreshTokenPurpose, plaintext);
+
+    private string DecryptAccess(Guid companyId, string ciphertext) =>
+        _fieldEncryption.Decrypt(companyId, AccessTokenPurpose, ciphertext);
+

diff --git a/docs/integrations/fortnox.md b/docs/integrations/fortnox.md
index 3c4d5e6..4d5e6f7 100644
--- a/docs/integrations/fortnox.md
+++ b/docs/integrations/fortnox.md
@@ -1,3 +1,5 @@
 # Fortnox integration
 
+OAuth state is stored server-side in the distributed cache and the browser only receives an opaque state handle. Access and refresh tokens are encrypted with the application field-encryption service before they are persisted in `fortnox_connections`, and all reads are scoped by `CompanyId`.
+
 Configure `FinanceIntegrations:Fortnox` with `Enabled`, `ClientId`, `ClientSecret`, `RedirectUri`, `AuthorizationUrl`, `TokenUrl`, `ApiBaseUrl`, and `Scopes`. When `Enabled=true`, startup validation fails if any required key is missing or URI values are not absolute HTTP or HTTPS URLs.
diff --git a/docs/runbooks/fortnox-integration.md b/docs/runbooks/fortnox-integration.md
index 4d5e6f7..5e6f708 100644
--- a/docs/runbooks/fortnox-integration.md
+++ b/docs/runbooks/fortnox-integration.md
@@ -1,3 +1,5 @@
 # Fortnox integration runbook
 
+Refreshes are coordinated with the application distributed lock provider using a company and connection scoped lock. If another node is already refreshing the same Fortnox connection, callers wait briefly, re-read the tenant-scoped token row, and then retry through the normal background retry path instead of issuing a second upstream refresh.
+
 Logs must never include access tokens, refresh tokens, authorization codes, client secrets, or raw Fortnox error payloads. Use company id, user id, connection id, safe status transitions, and HTTP status categories for diagnosis.
END_OF_PATCH
